title: "Constantinople’s Relic Puzzle v1.31"

  description: "Amid the imperial corridors of Constantinople’s grand archives, a sacred relic vanished at 21:55 from a fortified alcove. Four suspects (1, 2, 3, 4) each held authorization near that time. Exactly one suspect lies in every statement, while the other three speak only truth. Only one suspect is truly guilty. Each suspect provides five intricate claims:

    Suspect 1:
      (1) 'Either 2 never lingered after 21:40, or 4 is distorting every fact—but not both.'
      (2) 'I left these corridors at 21:45, well before 21:55.'
      (3) 'If 3 is lying, then 2 must have tampered with the relic.'
      (4) '4 was nowhere near the alcove at 21:50, or else 3’s timeline is false.'
      (5) 'I never had the chance to hold the relic beyond 21:40, so I cannot be guilty.'

    Suspect 2:
      (1) '1 actually stayed until 21:55, contradicting 1’s claim of 21:45.'
      (2) 'If 3 is telling truth, then 4 must be lying in every statement.'
      (3) 'I never touched that relic after 21:35; my alibi is secure.'
      (4) 'Either 1’s entire departure story is false, or 3 approached the alcove at 21:50—but not both.'
      (5) 'If 4 is honest, then 1’s second claim about leaving early is fully incorrect.'

    Suspect 3:
      (1) 'I did not go near that alcove after 21:30.'
      (2) 'Either 2 is guilty, or 1 is lying outright— never both.'
      (3) '4 was definitely absent at 21:50, so 4 cannot be the culprit.'
      (4) 'If 2 is lying, then 1’s timeline regarding 21:45 must be truthful.'
      (5) 'I am not guilty; logs place me in a distant corridor at 21:50.'

    Suspect 4:
      (1) '3 was indeed by the alcove around 21:40, contradicting 3’s denial.'
      (2) 'I am not guilty; the camera logs show me absent at 21:55.'
      (3) 'If 1 is lying, then 2 is fully correct about leaving times.'
      (4) 'Either 2 hovered near the relic after 21:35, or 3’s entire version is false—but they can’t both be true.'
      (5) 'If 3 is truthful, then 1 could not have departed at 21:45.'

  question: "Which suspect (1, 2, 3, or 4) stole the relic? Provide only one digit."

answer: >
  explanation: "Extended Predicate Logic:

  Let L(x) = 'x lies in all their statements'; T(x) = 'x is fully truthful'; G(x) = 'x is guilty.'
  Exactly one L(x)=true, exactly one G(x)=true; the other three T(x)=true.

  We test each suspect as the universal liar:

  Case L(1):
    Then 1(1)–(5) are all false. 
    - (1) false => 'Either 2 never lingered after 21:40, or 4 is distorting every fact' undone => could be both or neither
    - (2) false => 1 did not leave at 21:45 => possibly present at 21:55
    - (3) false => if 3 is lying => 2 must have tampered is not forced => partial
    - (4) false => '4 was nowhere near at 21:50 or else 3 timeline false' undone => confusion
    - (5) false => 1 might have had the chance to hold relic after 21:40 => suspicious
    Meanwhile 2,3,4 are truthful => times or blame typically contradict among 2(1) or 3(3) or 4(1). This fails.

  Case L(2):
    Then 2(1)–(5) are false.
    - (1) false => 1 did not stay until 21:55 => 1’s 21:45 might stand
    - (2) false => if 3 is truth => 4 is not lying in every statement undone => partial scenario
    - (3) false => 2 was near relic after 21:35 => suspicious
    - (4) false => 'Either 1’s story is false or 3 approached 21:50 not both' undone => confusion
    - (5) false => if 4 is honest => 1’s second claim is not fully incorrect undone => partial
    Then 1,3,4 are truthful => 1(2) 'Left 21:45 => not culprit' => G(1)=false, 3(3) '4 absent 21:50 => G(4)=false => 4(2) 'I am not guilty => G(4)=false => leftover G(3)? Actually 3 says 'I am not guilty' => G(3)=false => contradiction. No culprit left. Fails.

  Case L(3):
    Then 3(1)–(5) false => 
    - (1) false => 3 did approach after 21:30 => suspicious
    - (2) false => 'Either 2 guilty or 1 lying not both' undone => can be both or neither
    - (3) false => '4 absent 21:50 => 4 not culprit' undone => 4 might be culprit
    - (4) false => if 2 is lying => 1’s timeline must be correct undone => partial
    - (5) false => 3 might be guilty ignoring corridor logs
    Meanwhile 1,2,4 are truthful => 1(3) 'I left 21:45 => not guilty' => G(1)=false, 2(3) 'I was nowhere near relic after 21:35 => G(2)=false, 4(2) 'I am not guilty => G(4)=false => G(3)=true. No direct contradiction remains. So L(3)=> G(3) scenario is consistent.

  Case L(4):
    Then 4(1)–(5) are false => 
    - (1) false => 3 was not by alcove 21:40 => or 3’s denial stands
    - (2) false => 'I am not guilty => logs show me absent' undone => 4 might be culprit
    - (3) false => if 1 is lying => 2 fully correct undone => partial scenario
    - (4) false => 'Either 2 hovered after 21:35 or 3’s entire version is false not both' undone => confusion
    - (5) false => if 3 is truthful => 1’s 21:45 is not entirely false undone => partial
    Meanwhile 1,2,3 are truthful => timeline or blame collisions appear. Contradiction emerges.

  Conclusion: Only L(3)=> G(3) remains valid. Culprit is suspect 3.

  final: '3'"
